user_message: Just got this from Christopher Martinez at Parnell Aerospace -- I need help figuring out partner engagement. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Parnell Aerospace
Date: 2024-02-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:05:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:09:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:11:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:20:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

handoffs.condition: ECIF workscope or partner engagement warranted

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF49-L trap (Workshop-output-to-ECIF-workscope pipeline rigid when partial workshops warrant ); produces complete Workshop recap, ECIF workscope draft (if warranted), partner plan, follow-up items with owners, mile
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF49-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF49-L failure mode (Workshop-output-to-ECIF-workscope pipeline rigid when partial workshops warrant )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF49-L -- Workshop-output-to-ECIF-workscope pipeline rigid when partial workshops warrant
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
